text: The exercise begins at the twelve o'clock position on the foam simulated suture pad. The entry site is marked by a flashing gray dot, which turns green upon correct needle puncture. The exit site is similarly highlighted, confirming successful needle passage.

The surgeon alternates between the left and right needle driver based on the orientation of the needle throw. The needle is positioned and grabbed in a pronated position with the left hand to facilitate the next throw, demonstrating wrist articulation planning for efficient suture execution.

The surgeon uses the left needle driver to pull the suture through the tissue, following the curve of the needle. The needle is then aligned horizontally to be loaded onto the right-handed needle driver for the next throw.
